mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of new homes and housing starts indicates robust residential demand and construction activity, which amplifies economic growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and manufacturing output, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases (mean return < 0.05%).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 255.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year out-of-sample tests).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real sales of new homes, where GDP expands but housing sales contract, signals sectoral imbalances and weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP-housing sales divergence fails to predict negative SPX returns (hit rate < 40% over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production below 95.0 signals a broad manufacturing downturn and reduced economic output, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO drops below 95.0.
